content:
I also liked your point about the log being an obstacle Phoenix has to overcome. Another symbol that stood out to me was the paper windmill she decides to buy for her grandson. Even though she has almost nothing, she still wants to bring him something special. The windmill represents hope and happiness, showing that despite all her struggles, she still finds joy in thinking about her grandson’s future.

Your analysis of Phoenix’s journey as a symbol of life itself was really strong. The worn path represents all the hardships she has faced, but it also shows her strength. No matter how hard things get, she never stops moving forward. Your response really helped me see how deep the symbolism in the story goes, and I think Welty does an amazing job making Phoenix’s journey feel meaningful and inspiring.
